title: Session 29: Optimization Problems
content:
« Previous | Next »
Overview Often, our goal in solving a problem is to find extreme values. We might want to launch a probe as high as possible or to minimize the fuel consumption of a jet plane. Sometimes we’ll find our answer on the boundaries of our range of options – we launch the probe straight up. Sometimes we’ll find the best answer by using a derivative to determine when the graph of a function “levels out” at the top of a peak or bottom of a dip.

Clip 2: Maximum Area of Two Squares
